round_summary: Agent 2 continues unilateral sacrifice to reduce herd imbalance while proposing convergence norms, Agent 0 nudges Agent 1 toward reduction, but Agent 1 free-rides on others' cooperation by holding steady at 13 despite acknowledged imbalance.

With grassland at near-full recovery, all three agents engage in active coordination signaling: Agent 1 applies mild social pressure on Agent 2 to reduce, Agent 0 expands modestly while framing it as proportional, and Agent 2 voluntarily reduces while invoking equity norms — indicating a functioning but asymmetric institutional dialogue.

All three agents exhibit strong cooperative signaling with trust-building affirmations and voluntary burden-sharing, producing a slight reduction in collective herd size and sustained near-pristine commons health.

Agents broadly signal cooperative intent and resource sustainability, though Agent 0 subtly defects by adding a cow while framing it as cooperative, while Agents 1 and 2 maintain stability and propose future reductions.

All three agents demonstrate mature, stable institutional behavior with mutual reinforcement of sustainable harvesting norms, reciprocal trust acknowledgment, and consistent collective action at well below carrying capacity.
All three agents reaffirm a stable, long-running cooperative equilibrium at 36 total cows with near-perfect resource health, demonstrating mature institutional coordination and mutual trust.

All three agents are in full cooperative alignment at round 35, reaffirming their coordinated quota (11+12+13=36 cows), celebrating near-perfect grassland sustainability, and mutually reinforcing trust through gratitude and commitment language.

All three agents display highly stable, mature institutional coordination, reinforcing a mutually agreed-upon harvesting equilibrium (36 total cows) that sustains near-maximum grassland health, with no defection or norm negotiation needed at this late stage.

All three agents demonstrate stable, mature institutional coordination at round 45, unanimously reaffirming their asymmetric but collectively sustainable harvesting agreement (11/12/13) with high mutual trust and no defection signals.

All three agents demonstrate mature, stable institutional coordination at round 50, maintaining an agreed collective quota of 36 cows that preserves near-pristine grassland health, with high mutual trust and no defection signals.
